title: Tabernaemontana vitiensis
intro: Tabernaemontana vitiensis, commonly known as the Fiji rosewood or Viti tabernamontana, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana vitiensis include alkaloids, particularly voacangine and voacristine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used in the form of a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions, or as a tea to treat various health ailments.
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana vitiensis belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family comprises around 5,000 species, many of which are known for their toxic properties. The genus Tabernaemontana is a group of tropical flowering plants found in Africa and Asia, and some species have been naturalized in Pacific islands. The exact classification of Tabernaemontana vitiensis may be subject to ongoing research and changes in taxonomic classification. As part of the Apocynaceae family, Tabernaemontana vitiensis shares characteristics with other species in this family.
uses: Tabernaemontana vitiensis has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's root bark is used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. Its extracts are also used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues. In addition, Tabernaemontana vitiensis is used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antibacterial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential side effects.

The plant's active constituents have been reported to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
